recalls.Description: Umarex USA is recalling the T4E TR50 Air Pistol, a CO2-powered device that accommodates six .50 caliber rounds and is constructed from black plastic polymer. This air pistol, designed for recreational and training purposes, fires non-lethal ammunition such as rubber, plastic, or powder balls. Measuring approximately nine inches in length, it resembles a standard revolver. The affected model numbers include 2292112, 2280182, and 2280199, with the T4E logo and 'TR50' engraved on the left side of the muzzle, while the serial number is located on the right side.
recalls.Title: Umarex USA Issues Recall for T4E TR50 Air Pistols Due to Potential Impact Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Umarex at 877-212-1036 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email service@umarex.com, or visit http://t4eguns.com/recall or https://www.t4eguns.com/ and select 'Recall' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 30 reports of the CO2 cartridge unintentionally ejecting with force, including seven reports of injury, such as fractured hands, broken fingers and contusions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Big 5 stores across the Western United States, Modern Combat Sports in Vista, California, and online at https://mcsus.com, Amazon.com, and Valken.com from May 2022 to July 2023, priced around $110.

Importers.Name: Umarex USA, of Fort Smith, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of CO2 cartridge being forcefully ejected from the handle, creating an impact hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled air pistols and contact Umarex for a complimentary replacement and free return shipping for the recalled item.

recalls.metaDescription: Umarex recalls T4E TR50 Air Pistols due to impact hazard. Contact for free replacement and details.

recalls.Description: Crosman is recalling its Icon .177 and .22 Caliber Air Rifles due to a risk of injury. These PCP-powered, bolt-action air rifles are identified by model numbers CPI77S and CPI22S, which can be found on the left side of the rifle. The model name appears on the right side.
recalls.Title: Crosman Recalls Icon Air Rifles Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Crosman at 800-724-7486 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at www.crosman.com/iconrecall or by clicking on 'Recall' at the top of the Crosman homepage. You may also email iconrecall@crosman.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Crosman Icon .177 Caliber Air Rifle (CPI77S)
Recalled Crosman Icon .22 Caliber Air Rifle (CPI22S)
Recalled Crosman Model Number location on air rifle
The name of the model "icon" on the right-hand side of the rifle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an unintended discharge. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Do It Best Hardware, True Value, Service Star, and Sportsman's Guide stores nationwide, as well as online at www.Crosman.com, www.pyramydair.com, and www.Amazon.com from March 2022 through March 2024 for approximately $310.

Importers.Name: Crosman Corp., of Bloomfield, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of unexpected discharge if the air rifle is jolted or dropped

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled air rifles immediately and contact Crosman for a refund, which can be issued as store credit or a refund check after returning the air rifle. Crosman will provide a box with instructions and prepaid postage to those who register at www.crosman.com/iconrecall. The company is also reaching out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Crosman recalls Icon Air Rifles due to injury risk. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Ravin Crossbows is recalling eight models of the R500 series due to a safety concern. These crossbows measure approximately 8 inches wide when un-cocked and 28 inches long. Each model features a split limb design with cams positioned between the upper and lower limbs. Available in slate gray and camo, the crossbows come with a scope, quiver, cocking handle, quiver bracket, and six arrows. The Ravin logo and model names can be found on decals on the upper limbs, while the serial number is located on the left side of the rail above the trigger.
recalls.Title: Ravin R500 Series Crossbows Recalled Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ravin Crossbows at 888-298-6335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, email R500recall@ravincrossbows.com, or visit https://www.ravincrossbows.com/r500recall or www.ravincrossbows.com and navigate to "Safety & Instruction" then "Recall Notices" for further details.

Products.Name: Ravin R500 Crossbow
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,300 (In addition, about 425 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the crossbow unexpectedly discharging. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ravin Crossbows LLC, of Superior, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Ravin, Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, Dick's Sporting Goods, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.ravincrossbows.com from September 2021 through October 2023, priced between $2,550 and $4,025.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unexpected discharge when nearly cocked, posing an injury hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled crossbows and reach out to Ravin Crossbows for a complimentary repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Ravin recalls R500 series crossbows due to injury risk. Stop using immediately and contact for free repair.
